name: Covenant
description: God binds himself to his people by solemn promise: preservation to Noah, land and offspring and blessing to Abraham, law and belonging at Sinai, an everlasting throne to David, and the new covenant of the heart promised through Jeremiah and claimed at the Last Supper.

keyVerses.note: The covenant with Noah and every living creature — the bow in the clouds
The covenant with Abraham: land, offspring, blessing
Sinai: a kingdom of priests and a holy nation
The covenant with David: a throne established forever
The new covenant: law within, sins remembered no more
'This cup is the new covenant in my blood'

name: Redemption
description: God buys back what was lost: Israel out of Egypt by the blood of the lamb, Ruth by her kinsman-redeemer, the exiles out of Babylon, and — in the New Testament's proclamation — sinners by the blood of Christ.

keyVerses.note: 'I will redeem you with an outstretched arm'
Boaz the kinsman-redeemer
'I have redeemed you; I have called you by name'
The Son of Man came to give his life as a ransom for many
Redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of trespasses

name: Messiah
description: The anointed one promised across the whole canon: the seed of the woman, the blessing of Abraham, the prophet like Moses, the son of David, the suffering servant, the ruler from Bethlehem — a hope the New Testament proclaims fulfilled in Jesus.

keyVerses.note: The seed of the woman — the first promise
A prophet like Moses
A child born, government on his shoulder, David's throne forever
The ruler from Bethlehem
'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God'
'Beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted... the things concerning himself'

name: The Kingdom of God
description: God's reign asserted over all rival kingdoms: promised to David's line, dramatized in Daniel's visions of empires overthrown, announced by Jesus as 'at hand,' taught in parables, and consummated in the vision of Revelation.

keyVerses.note: 'The LORD will reign forever and ever' — the song at the sea
The LORD and his anointed against the raging nations
A kingdom that shall never be destroyed
'The kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe'
Mustard seed and leaven — the kingdom's hidden growth
'The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord'

name: The Presence of God
description: God dwelling with his people: Eden, the burning bush, the pillar of cloud and fire, the tabernacle and temple filled with glory, the glory departing before the exile, the Word made flesh who 'tabernacled' among us, the Spirit at Pentecost, and the city where God is all in all.

keyVerses.note: Holy ground at the burning bush
The glory fills the tabernacle
The cloud fills Solomon's temple
The glory departs — and returns
The Word became flesh and dwelt among us
'Behold, the dwelling place of God is with man'

name: Sacrifice and Atonement
description: Sin costs life, and God provides the substitute: the ram in Isaac's place, the Passover lamb, the Day of Atonement, the servant who bears the sin of many, and 'the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world.'

keyVerses.note: 'The LORD will provide' — the ram in Isaac's place
'When I see the blood, I will pass over you'
The Day of Atonement
'The LORD has laid on him the iniquity of us all'
'Behold, the Lamb of God'
Christ offered once to bear the sins of many

name: Resurrection and Life
description: God's power over death: sons restored by Elijah and Elisha, Israel's national resurrection in Ezekiel's valley of bones, Daniel's awakening of those who sleep in the dust, Lazarus called from the tomb, and the empty tomb on the third day — 'the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.'

keyVerses.note: 'I know that my Redeemer lives'
The valley of dry bones
Many who sleep in the dust shall awake
'I am the resurrection and the life'
Christ the firstfruits

name: Exile and Return
description: Banishment and homecoming as the Bible's recurring shape: Eden lost, Egypt and exodus, Babylon and the return under Cyrus — and the New Testament's claim that in Christ the true return from exile begins.

keyVerses.note: Driven from the garden — the first exile
Scattering and regathering foretold
'By the waters of Babylon... we wept'
Seventy years, then a future and a hope
Cyrus' decree: let him go up
The prodigal's far country and the father's welcome

name: Law and Grace
description: The commandments given at Sinai as covenant charter, Israel's long failure to keep them, and the promise of a law written on the heart — the tension the New Testament resolves in 'grace and truth through Jesus Christ.'

keyVerses.note: The Ten Commandments
The Shema: love the LORD with all your heart
'The law of the LORD is perfect, reviving the soul'
The law written within
Law through Moses; grace and truth through Jesus Christ
Justified by grace as a gift

name: Judgment and Mercy
description: The flood and the ark, Sodom and Lot's rescue, exile and return, Nineveh spared at Jonah's preaching: judgment announced, and mercy triumphing wherever there is repentance — 'in wrath remember mercy.'

keyVerses.note: 'Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?'
Merciful and gracious, yet by no means clearing the guilty
Jonah's complaint: 'I knew that you are a gracious God'
'In wrath remember mercy'
Mercy triumphs over judgment

name: Faith and Doubt
description: Abraham believing against hope, Israel doubting at Kadesh, Gideon's fleece, Elijah's despair, Peter sinking and denying, Thomas demanding to see — and faith counted as righteousness throughout.

keyVerses.note: 'He believed the LORD, and he counted it to him as righteousness'
'How long will they not believe in me?' — Kadesh
'The righteous shall live by his faith'
'I believe; help my unbelief!'
Thomas: 'blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed'
The roll call of faith

name: The Shepherd and the Flock
description: From the shepherd-king David and the LORD as shepherd of Psalm 23, through the failed shepherds of Ezekiel 34 and the promise that God himself will seek the lost, to Jesus the good shepherd who lays down his life for the sheep.

keyVerses.note: 'The LORD is my shepherd'
God himself will shepherd; 'my servant David' set over them
'Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered'
The shepherd who leaves the ninety-nine
'I am the good shepherd'
The chief Shepherd and the elders under him

name: The Remnant
description: God preserves a faithful few through every judgment: Noah's household, the seven thousand who never bowed to Baal, the survivors of exile who return, and the remnant chosen by grace of Paul's argument.

keyVerses.note: Only Noah was left, and those with him in the ark
'Yet I will leave seven thousand in Israel'
A remnant will return — Shear-jashub
'A brief moment of favor... a remnant'
A remnant, chosen by grace
